items.title: The Sky erased a 13-point first-quarter deficit, got 30 points from Sydney Taylor and forced overtime.
items.author: Drew
items.body_excerpt:
The Sky erased a 13-point first-quarter deficit, got 30 points from Sydney Taylor and forced overtime.

They still lost the rebounding battle 46-34, surrendered 19 offensive rebounds and allowed 29 points off 16 turnovers.

The fight was there. The margin for error wasn't.

items.title: In the Fever's OT win over the Sky, Caitlin Clark and Aliyah Boston became the first pair of teammates in WNBA history to each have a 30-point double-double in the same game.
items.author: Alexa Philippou
items.body_excerpt:
In the Fever's OT win over the Sky, Caitlin Clark and Aliyah Boston became the first pair of teammates in WNBA history to each have a 30-point double-double in the same game.

Boston: 34 points, 12 rebounds, 3 assists

Clark: 32 points, 10 assists, 7 boards

items.title: THAT’´ is the Chicago-Indiana rivalry I want to see.
items.author: Zena Keita
items.body_excerpt:
THAT’´ is the Chicago-Indiana rivalry I want to see. 

Give me more Caitlin Clark vs. Sydney Taylor. They were exceptional and so much fun to watch.

Aliyah played like an MVP, scoring at all levels and rebounding…and 100% from the line. 

AMAZING game.

Third time in triple digits this season; first win.

items.title: Fever built a huge early lead, struggled for 15 minutes, then found just enough footing from their stars. It wasn't pretty at all, and it took overtime. But they beat the Sly 1114-106 and win their second game in a row. They're 7-5. Clark and Boston with over 30 points each.
items.author: Tony East
items.body_excerpt: Fever built a huge early lead, struggled for 15 minutes, then found just enough footing from their stars. It wasn't pretty at all, and it took overtime. But they beat the Sly 1114-106 and win their second game in a row. They're 7-5. Clark and Boston with over 30 points each.

items.title: The Fever hang on against the Sky in OT, 114-106, to improve to 5-2 at home.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
The Fever hang on against the Sky in OT, 114-106, to improve to 5-2 at home.

Fever had a troubling middle two quarters, giving up 65pts (and 33pts combined in the 1st & 4th). Boston had a career-high 34/12, Clark added 32-7-10. Fever 29/31 on FTs.

Next: Sat. at CON.

-They almost blew it v BOS
-Who deserved Finals MVP?

media.title: How the Knicks attacked (behind) Victor Wembanyama to put New York one win away from NBA title
media.description: The Knicks didn't just find their offense in the second half of Game 4, for large stretches, they actively looked for the Defensive Player of the Year to do so.

items.title: Caitlin Clark, patient. Holds her handle in the lane long enough to draw in the defense, then fires a perfect pass to a cutting Aliyah Boston. Ties the game at 81. 7:50 to decide this game.
items.author: Tony East
items.body_excerpt: Caitlin Clark, patient. Holds her handle in the lane long enough to draw in the defense, then fires a perfect pass to a cutting Aliyah Boston. Ties the game at 81. 7:50 to decide this game.

items.title: Sky guard Sydney Taylor has erupted for 16 of her 25pts in the 3rd and has made 9 of 10 shots off the bench. Everything is going down. And she got a steal to end the quarter.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
Sky guard Sydney Taylor has erupted for 16 of her 25pts in the 3rd and has made 9 of 10 shots off the bench. Everything is going down. And she got a steal to end the quarter.

Indiana trailing 79-72 after yielding a 39-point 3Q from Chicago.

items.title: Taylor finishes with 16 points in just the third quarter. She's scored four combined across her previous four games, though she did have 27 against Toronto earlier this season. She's at 25 right now.
items.author: Tony East
items.body_excerpt:
